title: Lydia Daybed
summary: A moulding-trimmed daybed with tapered end legs, scalloped MDF side panels, and a full slat platform between two panelled end frames.
description:
The decorative payoff here is in the moulding and the scalloped side panels — the underlying structure is a straightforward pair of panelled end frames joined by two long siderails, with slats resting across the top. Taper the end legs and side legs with a jigsaw before assembly (a small taper is all it takes; both sets of legs taper, so keep them consistent with each other), since it's much easier to true up a taper on a flat board than after the frame is built.

Cut one scalloped side panel from the layout points given, then use that finished piece as a physical template for the remaining three — tracing a proven pattern keeps all four sides matching, which freehand marking each one separately won't reliably do.

tools.note: Tapering the legs and cutting the scalloped side panels.

steps.title: Build the end panels
steps.body: Use 1-1/4" nails and glue to fasten the 1x2 end legs to the end panels, keeping the side and top edges flush. Taper the legs with a jigsaw — just a slight taper is enough, and keep it consistent since the side legs will also be tapered.

steps.title: Add the bottom panel trim
steps.body: Use 1-1/4" nails and glue to fasten the bottom panel trim in place, keeping the bottom edges flush.

steps.title: Add the side legs
steps.body: Use glue and 2" nails to fasten the tapered side legs to the panel and the end legs, nailing all the way down the end leg.

steps.title: Attach the tops
steps.body: Attach the tops of the ends with 2" nails and glue, keeping edges flush with the side legs.

steps.title: Cut and attach the panel trim
steps.body: Cut the outside and inside moulding trim to fit the end panels and attach with glue and nails.

steps.title: Cut the scalloped sides
steps.body: Use the layout points given to draw the arched, scalloped shape and cut out with a jigsaw. Once one side is cut, use it as a template for the remaining three.

steps.title: Attach the scalloped sides
steps.body: Drill pocket holes in the scalloped side panels and attach to the siderails. Then use 1-1/4" nails and glue to attach the cleats to the siderails.

steps.title: Assemble the bed and add the slats
steps.body: Assemble the end panels with the siderails, then lay the slats on top of the siderails and screw down with 1-1/4" screws.

steps.title: Add the siderail trim
steps.body: Measure and cut the siderail trim to fit, then glue and nail it to the siderails with 1-1/4" nails.

images.alt: A moulding-trimmed daybed with tapered legs and scalloped side panels
